title: Sacred Heli-Yatra to Mount Kailash and Lake Mansarovar

activities.description: Upon arriving at Tribhuvan International Airport (KTM), meet your Max Holidays representative and transfer to your luxury hotel in Kathmandu. Take time to relax and recover from your flight.
activities.location: Tribhuvan International Airport to Kathmandu Hotel

activities.description: Meet your fellow pilgrims and travel guides for a thorough briefing on high-altitude safety, essential packing, and spiritual preparations for the holy journey ahead.
Attend a morning prayer service and receive blessings at Pashupatinath, one of the most sacred Hindu temples dedicated to Lord Shiva, located on the banks of the Bagmati River.
Explore the majestic Buddhist stupas of Kathmandu. Walk around the giant dome of Boudhanath, spinning the prayer wheels and absorbing the serene chanting.
Board a short domestic flight to Nepalgunj, located in the Terai region near the Indian border. This acts as the key staging ground for the flight to the mountains.
Check in to your hotel and conduct a final check of your warm clothing, trekking gear, and medical kits. Stay well hydrated.

days.theme: Scenic Flight to Simikot and Crossing to Taklakot

activities.description: Fly early to Simikot (2,910m) enjoying bird's-eye views of the rugged Himalayan peaks. Transition directly to a helicopter to fly to the border outpost of Hilsa.

activities.activity: Tibet Border Crossing & Drive to Taklakot
activities.description: Cross the Nepal-China border bridge at Hilsa. Complete immigration formalities and board your Tibetan luxury coach for a scenic drive to Taklakot (Purang) at 4,025m.
To adapt to the high elevation, take a light, slow-paced walk around the historical town of Taklakot. Explore local markets and observe the unique mix of Tibetan and Nepalese cultures.
Your tour leader will conduct oxygen level and heart rate checks. Rest for the remainder of the evening to prepare your lungs for the higher altitudes of the trek.

days.theme: First Divine Glimpse & Holy Bath at Lake Mansarovar

activities.description: Drive past the dark, crescent-shaped Rakshas Taal (Lake of the Demon) before crossing over to the sacred, sun-shaped freshwater Lake Mansarovar (4,590m). Enjoy your first majestic view of Mt. Kailash.
Participate in a group havan (holy fire ritual) and take a holy splash or bath using the pristine, cold waters of Lake Mansarovar to purify your mind, body, and soul.
Visit the historic Chiu Monastery built on a steep cliffside on the northwest shore of Lake Mansarovar, offering panoramic views of the brilliant sapphire water.

days.theme: Drive to Darchen & Trek to Dirapuk (Parikrama Day 1)

activities.activity: Drive to Darchen and Yam Dwar
activities.description: Drive to Darchen, the base camp of Mount Kailash, and continue to Yam Dwar (Tarboche flagpole), the auspicious gateway marking the physical start of the 52-kilometer trek.
Begin your 12 km foot pilgrimage alongside the Lha Chu River. The path offers stunning perspectives of Mount Kailash's western and northern faces. Hire a pony or porter if pre-arranged.
Marvel at the spectacular golden sunset lighting up the sheer northern face of the diamond-shaped Mount Kailash directly in front of the Dirapuk guesthouse.

days.theme: Crossing the Dolma La Pass to Zuthulpukh (Parikrama Day 2)

activities.activity: Pre-Dawn Ascent to Dolma La Pass
activities.description: Begin the toughest phase of the trek (22 km total). Walk slowly and steadily up the steep climb to Dolma La Pass, situated at a challenging elevation of 5,630 meters.
Gaze down at the emerald-green holy lake of Gauri Kund (the Lake of Compassion) located just below the pass. Carefully descend the rocky pathways down into the eastern valley.
activities.location: Dolma La to Gauri Kund Descent

activities.description: Reach the Zuthulpukh guesthouse (4,760m) named after the miraculous cave of Yogi Milarepa. Rest your exhausted limbs and enjoy warm herbal tea.
Trek a gentle 9 km down the valley along beautiful flat terrain flanked by dramatic rocky cliffs. Meet your support vehicles waiting at the end point of the trail.
Board the coaches for a relaxing drive back to Taklakot. Enjoy a celebratory hot lunch and a long-awaited hot shower at the hotel.

days.theme: Retracing Steps: Tibet to Nepalgunj & Kathmandu

activities.description: Drive back to the Hilsa border, cross into Nepal, and board the helicopter back to Simikot Airport.
Catch your domestic flight from Simikot to Nepalgunj, and immediately connect to your final flight back to Kathmandu. Check-in to your luxury hotel for a comfortable night.
Spend your final morning shopping for spiritual souvenirs such as Rudraksha beads, Shaligram stones, singing bowls, and local Tibetan prayer flags in Thamel.
With your mind refreshed and your spirit deeply rejuvenated, transfer to Tribhuvan International Airport for your flight back home.

additionalNotes: PACKING SUGGESTIONS: High-quality down jacket, thermal innerwear layers, sturdy broke-in trekking boots, sunscreen, UV sunglasses, trekking poles, and personal medical kit. ALTITUDE SICKNESS: Drink 3-4 liters of water daily. Consult a doctor beforehand for Diamox or altitude prescriptions. PASSPORT & VISA: Ensure your passport has at least 6 months validity. Foreigners and NRIs must acquire a China Group Visa in Kathmandu, requiring a physical passport.

title: Sacred Pilgrimage: 11-Day Helicopter Kailash Mansarovar Yatra
destination: Mount Kailash and Lake Mansarovar, Tibet (via Kathmandu, Nepal)

days.theme: Arrival in the City of Temples

activities.description: Upon arrival at Tribhuvan International Airport (KTM) in Kathmandu, meet your tour representative and transfer to your luxury hotel. Check in, settle into your room, and receive your welcome kit.
activities.location: Tribhuvan International Airport to Hyatt Regency Kathmandu

activities.description: Attend a mandatory pre-yatra orientation. Our medical officer will conduct a basic physiological check-up, measuring blood pressure and oxygen saturation levels to assess high-altitude readiness.
Enjoy a nutritious, light vegetarian buffet dinner featuring Nepalese specialties. Drink plenty of water to start your hydration routine before ascending to higher altitudes.

days.theme: Spiritual Blessings & Journey to the Plains

activities.description: Fuel up with a wholesome international buffet breakfast focusing on high-carbohydrate options to prepare for travel.
Embark on a sacred visit to the famous Pashupatinath Temple, a highly revered UNESCO World Heritage Site dedicated to Lord Shiva. Seek blessings for a successful and safe yatra.
Transfer to Kathmandu domestic airport and board a scheduled flight to Nepalgunj, a city situated in the lowlands near the Indian border.
Arrive at your hotel in Nepalgunj. Relish a healthy, freshly prepared Indian vegetarian dinner. Rest early to prepare for tomorrow's mountain flights.
Take a scenic, early morning twin-otter aircraft flight from Nepalgunj to Simikot (2,910m), enjoying aerial views of the deep Himalayan valleys.

activities.activity: Helicopter Hop to Hilsa & Border Crossing
activities.description: Board a chartered helicopter for a dramatic flight to Hilsa, the border outpost. Cross the friendship bridge on foot, clear Chinese immigration, and meet your Tibetan guide and deluxe coach.
Embark on a scenic drive to Purang (Taklakot), winding through rocky Tibetan landscapes at 3,900m. Check into your hotel to let your body adjust to the sudden altitude shift.
Enjoy a hot soup and an authentic, low-sodium vegetarian dinner designed by our specialized kitchen crew to aid altitude acclimatization.

days.theme: Acclimatization in the Cradle of Mountains

activities.description: Have a relaxed breakfast featuring warm porridge, herbal tea, and fresh fruits.
Engage in a gentle walking excursion around the local Purang market and nearby hills. This light cardiovascular exercise helps trigger blood oxygenation.
Savor a warm lunch. Afterward, participate in our group medical session where oxygen levels are monitored and hydration targets are checked.
Spend the afternoon resting. End your day with a comforting dinner before packing light bags for the upcoming lake and kora trek.

days.theme: The Mystic Shorelines of Lake Mansarovar

activities.description: Begin a beautiful drive through the high passes. En route, make a stop at Rakshas Taal (the lake of demons) for breathtaking photographs of its dark blue, curved shorelines.

activities.activity: First Glimpse of Mount Kailash & Holy Bath
activities.description: Arrive at the banks of the sapphire-blue Lake Mansarovar (4,590m) and witness the spectacular view of Mount Kailash. Perform a holy splash of water (or a quick bath) and sit for a peaceful puja.
Savor hot rice, lentils, and steamed vegetables prepared by our kitchen team at the lakeside campsite.
Visit Chiu Monastery, beautifully carved into a cliff overlooking the lake. Watch the sunset illuminate Mount Kailash in shades of gold.
Sip hot herbal soups and have dinner at the lakeside guesthouse. Keep warm as nighttime temperatures fall dramatically.

days.theme: The Beginning of the Holy Kora

activities.description: Witness the divine morning ambience of Lake Mansarovar. Eat a heavy, warm breakfast to boost energy levels for the trek ahead.
Drive to Darchen, the base camp of Mount Kailash, and continue to Yam Dwar (the Gate of Salvation). This is the official starting point of the physical Parikrama.

activities.activity: Trek from Yam Dwar to Dirapuk
activities.description: Begin your 12 km trek alongside the rushing Lha-Chu river. Marvel at the dramatic, sheer cliffs of the western face of Mount Kailash.
Stop for a dry-packed lunch of energy bars, nuts, cheese, and fruits, keeping hydrated with warm water from your thermos.
Arrive at Dirapuk (4,860m). Stand in awe of the massive, diamond-shaped north face of Mount Kailash, glowing under the late afternoon sun.
Enjoy a simple, piping hot dinner in the communal dining hall. Sleep under heavy layers to battle the sub-zero high-altitude night.

days.theme: The Ultimate Test: Over Dolma La Pass

activities.description: Eat a quick, warm meal. Equip your headlamps, trekking poles, and layers for the most challenging day of the yatra.
Embark on a steep, demanding climb of 6 km. Reach the highest point of the trek, Dolma La Pass, decorated with thousands of colorful Tibetan prayer flags.
Carefully descend from the pass. View the beautiful, emerald-green, frozen waters of Gauri Kund (the Lake of Compassion) nestled in the valley below.
Enjoy a hot noodle soup or packed snacks at a temporary tea tent situated along the descent trail.
Conclude the long 22 km trek day by arriving at the peaceful campsite of Zuthulpukh, where the cave of miracles of Milarepa is located.
Relish a well-earned hot dinner with your fellow pilgrims. Share stories of crossing the formidable pass.

activities.activity: Final Trek Leg to Mani Walls
activities.description: Complete the last 6 km of the Parikrama on a flat, gentle trail lined with ancient Mani stones and stupas.

activities.activity: Reunion and Drive back to Purang
activities.description: Meet the support vehicles at the end of the trekking trail. Board the coaches and drive back to Purang, bidding a spiritual farewell to Mount Kailash.
Celebrate the successful completion of the outer Parikrama with a warm, multi-course Indian vegetarian lunch.
Enjoy a long-awaited hot shower and spend the evening relaxing your sore muscles.
Enjoy a nourishing hot dinner and a restful night's sleep in a warm, comfortable room.
Depart early from Purang, complete Chinese exit immigration at the Tibet border, and cross the suspension bridge back into Nepal at Hilsa.
Board the helicopter shuttles back to Simikot, enjoying a swift escape from the high desert of Tibet back to Nepal's lush green valleys.
Board your domestic fixed-wing flight from Simikot to Nepalgunj, descending rapidly to the warm plains.
Settle back into the comforts of your premium hotel. Celebrate your return with a grand vegetarian dinner.
Board a morning flight from Nepalgunj back to the capital city of Kathmandu. Upon landing, transfer back to your premium hotel.
Enjoy a leisurely lunch at the hotel, followed by an open afternoon for souvenir shopping, visiting Thamel, or resting.
Gather for a celebratory dinner with your group. Receive your official Kailash Mansarovar Yatra completion certificate from Max Holidays.
Enjoy a final hearty breakfast and pack your bags. Collect your passport and travel documents.
Board the private airport transfer vehicle to catch your international flight back home, taking with you memories of a lifetime and an inner peace.

additionalNotes: PACKING ESSENTIALS: Thermal innerwear, windproof/waterproof down jacket, sturdy broken-in trekking boots, heavy gloves, sunglasses with UV protection, wide-brimmed hat, thermos water bottle, and a personal medical kit containing Altitude Sickness medication (Acetazolamide/Diamox) as prescribed by your physician. IMPORTANT: Carry your physical, valid passport and original Indian Voter ID (if applicable). Prioritize cardiovascular exercises (walking/jogging) for at least 4-6 weeks before starting the yatra. Keep in mind that helicopter and flights are highly weather-dependent, so always remain flexible to minor schedule delays.

title: Sacred Ascent: 11-Day Helicopter Kailash Mansarovar Pilgrimage
destination: Mount Kailash and Lake Mansarovar, Tibet Autonomous Region, China

days.theme: Welcome to the Valley of Gods

activities.description: Arrive at Tribhuvan International Airport in Kathmandu, where our local representative will greet you and transfer you to your hotel in a private air-conditioned vehicle.
Meet our professional mountaineering experts for a critical safety briefing, details on altitude acclimatization, and distribution of your high-altitude gear bags and down jackets.
Enjoy a warm vegetarian Nepali feast with traditional cultural dance performances to kick off your spiritual journey.
Enjoy a hearty breakfast with options loaded with complex carbohydrates to prepare for the upcoming altitude.
Embark on a sacred visit to Pashupatinath Temple, one of the most revered Shiva temples. Participate in a special prayer service for a successful and safe Yatra.
Savor local organic dishes followed by a final review of your medical certificates, passports, and Chinese visa documentation.
Stroll through the vibrant streets of Thamel for last-minute shopping of essential items like walking sticks, thermal wear, and energy bars.
Board a scenic domestic flight south to Nepalgunj, located close to the Indian border. The air here is warmer and denser, offering a comfortable resting place.
Settle into your premium rooms and enjoy a healthy North Indian vegetarian lunch buffet designed to keep hydration high.
Visit the historic Bageshwari Temple dedicated to Goddess Durga, housing a unique temple of Lord Shiva with a mustache.

days.theme: Over the Mighty Himalayas to Tibet Border

activities.description: Take an early morning fixed-wing flight to Simikot (2,910m), soaring past deep Himalayan valleys and dramatic cliffs.
Board a chartered helicopter from Simikot for an incredibly scenic mountain flight over narrow gorges directly to the Nepalese border town of Hilsa.
Cross the border bridge on foot, complete Chinese immigration formalities, and drive via deluxe tourist buses to Taklakot (Purang) at 4,025m in the Tibet Autonomous Region.
Indulge in a light, hot vegetarian dinner and sleep early to let your body adjust to the thin mountain air.
An easy-paced walk around the local Tibetan town to stimulate red blood cell production. Remember to drink plenty of warm water.
Visit the beautiful, centuries-old Buddhist monastery carved into the hillside and admire the panoramic views of the Sarayu River below.
A comforting meal consisting of garlic soup, steamed vegetables, and lentils to help prevent acute mountain sickness.
Drive through spectacular high-altitude desert roads. Pause at Rakshas Taal, the dark saltwater lake symbolizing shadow, before reaching the brilliant freshwater Lake Mansarovar.
Reach the pristine shores of Lake Mansarovar (4,590m). Perform a sacred holy bath and a special Vedic Hawan ritual with the grand silhouette of Mt. Kailash reflecting in the water.
A cozy evening close to the sacred waters. Keep warm and rest in preparation for the upcoming parikrama.

activities.activity: Drive to Yam Dwar via Darchen
activities.description: Drive to Darchen and proceed onwards to Yam Dwar, the magnificent stone gate symbolizing the Gateway of the God of Death. Passing through clears the path for the holy circuit.
Begin the physical 13 km trek along the western face of Mount Kailash. The path is moderately flat, tracing the Lha Chu river canyon.
Arrive at Dirapuk (4,860m) and witness the breathtaking sunset turning the North Face of Mount Kailash into a glowing golden pyramid.
Embark on the most challenging portion of the trek, climbing steep, rocky steps up to the highest point of the Yatra at 5,630 meters.

activities.activity: Prays at Dolma La & Gauri Kund Darshan
activities.description: Reach the pass adorned with countless colorful Tibetan prayer flags. Descend slightly to view Gauri Kund, the turquoise lake of compassion associated with Goddess Parvati.
Follow a long, gradual downhill path along the Zhong Chu valley to reach the peaceful campsite of Zuthulpukh (4,760m).
Complete the last 6 km of the spiritual circuit. The trail is gentle and lined with beautifully carved Buddhist Mani stones.
Bid farewell to Mount Kailash, meet your support vehicles, and drive back down to the lower, oxygen-rich altitudes of Taklakot.
Savor a grand celebratory vegetarian dinner with your fellow Yatris, sharing stories of faith and endurance from the trek.

activities.activity: Return Border Crossing & Heli to Simikot
activities.description: Cross the border back to Nepal at Hilsa and immediately board your helicopter shuttle over the green mountains to Simikot.
Fly to Nepalgunj, then transition smoothly to your afternoon connecting flight to Kathmandu.
Check back into your comfortable hotel and enjoy a peaceful gourmet meal with a warm shower and soft bed.
Collect sacred water from Lake Mansarovar, prayer wheels, and brass statues of Lord Shiva from local Thamel shops as lifelong keepsakes.
Transfer to the international airport in a private car for your onward journey, filled with deep spiritual satisfaction.

additionalNotes: Pack warm, layered clothing including windproof jackets, thermal innerwear, and broken-in waterproof hiking boots. Standard age limit for the Tibet travel permit is up to 70 years. Maintain steady hydration, avoid alcohol/smoking, and pack altitude sickness medications like Diamox under medical advice. Ensure your passport is valid for at least six months from the date of travel.
